executed_by: test-pc (autonomous /loop polling, windows-mcp browser automation, anonymous session)
description: v0.app Option E pre-flight probe. Verified whether the /chat/<newChatId> navigation triggered by submitting a prompt is an SSR top-level document request or a client-side pushState()/RSC navigation.

results.verdict: CSR_PUSHSTATE — Option E (block page substitution at /chat/<id>) is BLOCKED

login_state.evidence: Sign In + Sign Up links still visible in top-right nav after navigation; 3 /api/chat/chat/attributes, /api/chat/chat/template, /api/git/connection calls all 'Fetch request failed' for anon user. Same anonymous flow as #447.

rows_matching_chat_substring.name: send
rows_matching_chat_substring.note: main chat API — the existing APF block point from #438

rows_matching_chat_substring.name: connection?chatId=hJ35MSWtyWu
rows_matching_chat_substring.note: git integration init for the new chat id

rows_matching_chat_substring.name: chats?project=draft&_rsc=l5937
rows_matching_chat_substring.note: sidebar chat LIST refetch — plural /chats not /chat/<id>. Not the detail route.

rows_matching_hJ35MSWtyWu_document_type.verdict_detail: ZERO document-type requests for /chat/hJ35MSWtyWu. ZERO RSC fetches for /chat/hJ35MSWtyWu?_rsc=... either. The new chat route URL appeared in the address bar purely via history.pushState() — the detail route was rendered entirely by client-side React state (Next.js App Router with 'use client' components).

document_filter_check.action_taken: Also switched the Type filter to 'Doc' (Document only) before widening to All
document_filter_check.result: Doc filter showed essentially an empty list during the submit→navigate window (only the original v0.app landing document from the initial page load, which had already been cleared by Clear network log button before submit)
document_filter_check.implication: Confirms the submit→navigate action produced no new document-level request

native_v0_behavior_observed: Chat bubble renders immediately with user text (React state update)
URL bar shows v0.app/chat/hJ35MSWtyWu (pushState, no network request)
In parallel, multiple Fetch calls fire: rate-limit, send, send-site, integrations, git/connection, chat/attributes, chat/template
Most anon-only calls fail (attributes, template, git/connection 1st attempt)
The chat page as a new route is NOT refetched from the server at any point — it's purely in-memory React navigation

ssr_markers_check.action_taken: Skipped because no document response body exists to inspect
ssr_markers_check.alternative_evidence: The original https://v0.app landing page (root /) IS an SSR Next.js document (loaded at page open, __NEXT_DATA__ present implicitly by Next.js convention), but the /chat/<id> sub-route is handled purely client-side from that landing page's JS bundle. This is the standard Next.js App Router 'parallel route + client transition' pattern.

results.phase6_recommendation: Option E blocked — Option F is the only remaining viable path

option_e_classic_block_page_at_slash_cha.reason: No document request for /chat/<id> ever happens. APF has nothing to intercept at that URL. Serving HTML at /chat/<id> would only take effect on a fresh page reload or direct URL entry — both of which are outside the normal user flow.

option_e_prime_rsc_intercept.reason: Would require APF to intercept the `/chats?project=draft&_rsc=l5937` sidebar refetch (or a hypothetical /chat/<id>?_rsc=... if such a fetch were to happen on direct navigation) and inject a forged React Server Component payload containing warning UI. RSC wire format is internal Next.js and changes between versions — brittle.
option_e_prime_rsc_intercept.additional_concern: The sidebar /chats RSC is for the chat LIST not the chat detail, so injecting there would put the warning in the sidebar, not the main chat area.

option_f_303_redirect_from_send.reason: v0 calls POST /chat/api/send with the user prompt. APF already controls this endpoint (it's where the JSON block body was served in #438). Instead of returning `{error: true, message: ...}` JSON (which v0 ignores), APF can return HTTP 303 See Other with Location: https://etap-warning.example/apf-blocked?service=v0. The browser will follow the 303, issuing a GET to the Etap-controlled warning page — which IS a document request APF fully owns, and the warning HTML renders as a top-level page.
requires: Etap must host a standalone /apf-blocked HTML page (or route) that renders the warning text, preferably with a back-button to v0

Verify that v0's fetch() call to /send handles 303 correctly — the frontend's fetch() may or may not auto-follow cross-origin redirects. If fetch() swallows the 303 silently, the browser window itself needs to navigate; a `window.location.assign()` trigger is NOT available to APF (server-side only), so the fallback is to return a 200 text/html document with a <script>top.location='...'</script> or <meta http-equiv='refresh'> — fetch() will receive the HTML but the JS/meta won't execute because fetch is an XHR, not a navigation.
CONCLUSION: Option F in its pure 303 form is UNLIKELY to cause a top-level navigation because /chat/api/send is called via fetch(), not via <form submit>. Need to verify with a test.

option_g_new_error_recovery_hook.reason: v0 has Sentry error reporting. When fetch() to /chat/api/send fails with a non-2xx that it can't parse, the frontend MAY have an error recovery path that navigates somewhere. Test: return HTTP 403 with Content-Type: text/html body containing <meta http-equiv='refresh' url='https://etap-warning...'> — see if v0's error handler calls location.assign() on 403.
option_g_new_error_recovery_hook.risk: Depends on v0 frontend code we don't control.

option_h_document_on_direct_navigation.verdict: POSSIBLE — covers the 'reload after block' case only
option_h_document_on_direct_navigation.reason: If a user reloads the page after being blocked, the browser WILL issue a GET document request to https://v0.app/chat/<id> (because the URL is now in the history). APF can intercept THAT document request and return HTML. This covers the reload scenario but not the initial block — the initial block is still BLOCK_ONLY until the user reloads.
option_h_document_on_direct_navigation.use_case: Pair with Option F attempt: Option F tries to show warning on initial block, Option H guarantees the warning shows on reload.

action_items_for_dev: Update services/v0_design.md: Option E marked BLOCKED (CSR navigation confirmed), primary strategy switches to Option F (303 redirect from /send) WITH the caveat that fetch() may not follow redirects for navigation — test required
If Option F proves insufficient, fall back to Option H (block-page at direct /chat/<id> document requests, which only helps on reload)
Consider Option G (HTTP 403 + meta-refresh body) as an experimental middle ground

NONE of the options gives guaranteed first-try visible warning. v0's pure-CSR architecture is fundamentally hostile to HTTP-layer warning injection. The cleanest long-term option may be an authenticated SSE injection (back to Option A from #447) which would require Vercel login to reverse-engineer the event envelope.

key_insights.nextjs_app_router_is_pure_csr_for_submit: v0.app uses Next.js App Router where a prompt submit triggers React state update + pushState(), not a server navigation. The URL change is cosmetic — no new document, no RSC refetch for the detail route. APF cannot intercept something that never goes over the network.
key_insights.rsc_fetches_exist_but_not_for_new_route: There IS an RSC fetch in the log (`/chats?project=draft&_rsc=l5937`) but it's for the sidebar chat list, NOT for /chat/<newId>. The new chat detail view is rendered purely from the already-loaded JS bundle.
key_insights.option_e_precondition_was_wrong: #447 design assumed /chat/<id> is a document navigation because the URL changed. That assumption failed — the URL changes via pushState, not via server navigation. This is a generic risk for any SPA service: URL changes ≠ document requests.
key_insights.only_intercept_point_is_chat_api_send: The only network calls APF can influence for v0 are /chat/api/send, /chat/api/send-site, /chat/api/integrations, and the /api/chat/* family. None of these carry a top-level document response. Warning delivery has to work through one of these fetch() endpoints or not at all.
key_insights.pending_infra_candidate: If none of Options F/G/H work in test, v0 should be classified as PENDING_INFRA (awaiting SSE envelope reverse-engineering with an authenticated session) rather than DONE.

overall_conclusion: is_document_request: FALSE. v0 uses Next.js App Router with pure client-side navigation for the prompt-submit flow. Option E (block page substitution at /chat/<id>) is BLOCKED because the URL change is history.pushState(), not a server navigation. Recommended pivots: Option F (303 redirect from /chat/api/send, with caveat that fetch() may not follow), Option H (cover reload case with direct /chat/<id> document interception), and long-term Option A (SSE envelope injection, needs authenticated session). services/v0_design.md requires revision.
